title: Vintage Step Stool
summary: A two-step wooden stool with jigsaw-shaped 1x12 sides and slatted 1x8 treads — a small, sturdy classic step stool for a kitchen or closet.
description:
This step stool gets its vintage look entirely from its shaped sides — cut both from a single 1x12, using the first cut piece as a pattern for the second so the two match exactly, rather than measuring and cutting them independently. Take the jigsaw cuts slowly and note which side of the traced line to cut on; sanding the two edges afterward is what makes a pair of hand-cut sides read as a matched set rather than two slightly different shapes.

Once the sides are shaped and sanded to match, the bottom supports go on first, tying the two sides together into the stool's basic footprint, followed by the top supports built the same way. **Pocket holes keep every joint clean and hidden**, though the plan notes a countersunk 2" wood screw works as a simple substitute if you don't have a jig.

The two tread boards rest on the assembled supports last, giving the stool its two step heights. This is a genuinely fast, low-material build — a good weekend project for a scrap 1x12 and a short 1x8 offcut.

materials.name: Self-tapping star bit screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Shaped with a jigsaw; cut one first and use as a pattern for the other.

steps.title: Cut and shape the sides
steps.body: Cut the two side pieces from the 1x12 with a jigsaw, using the diagram as a guide. Use the first cut piece as a pattern to trace and cut the second. Sand both edges so they match.

steps.title: Attach the bottom supports
steps.body: Attach the bottom supports between the two sides with screws and glue, using pocket hole screws or 2" wood screws with a countersink.
materials: Self-tapping star bit screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Attach the top supports
steps.body: Attach the top supports to the sides the same way as the bottom supports.
materials: Self-tapping star bit screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Attach the treads
steps.body: Attach the two tread boards on top of the assembled supports with screws and glue.
materials: Self-tapping star bit screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Fill any screw holes with wood filler, sand all surfaces, and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly
